metadata.label: gen2/AppliedIntuition-CloudInfra
metadata.title: Software Engineer - Cloud Infrastructure
metadata.team_direction: is a leading company in the field of physical AI, creating the digital infrastructure necessary for intelligent machines

revision_priority: 修正 Summary 第 3 句及 DiDi Project blockquote 中 "**2022 city**" / "**2023 city**" / "**20+ city**" 的加粗范围，将 "city" 移出粗体，仅保留数字加粗。
修订 Summary 首句 "3+ years building Go, Java, and Python services" 措辞，明确 Go 资历仅限 TikTok 实习阶段（6 个月），避免 HR 对照时间线时产生可信度疑问。

findings.issue: JD core skills explicitly call out GCP, but the resume only evidences AWS and Azure. For a cloud infrastructure role, this leaves one core cloud-provider signal unclosed.
findings.fix: If there is any real GCP work, add it to the most relevant cloud/deployment bullet with the exact service or deployment context; otherwise keep the resume anchored on AWS/Azure and do not imply direct GCP ownership.

findings.issue: The newest experience opens with an outcome metric, but it lacks a concrete scope anchor such as service count, traffic volume, or rollout scale. That makes the cloud-infra story feel more like a small-team improvement than a senior infrastructure signal.
findings.fix: Add one truthful scale cue to the same bullet, such as number of services, weekly rollout volume, or workflow volume, so the first impression is anchored in scope instead of only percentage improvement.

revision_priority: [SHOULD] Close the GCP gap in the Cloud skill story by adding a truthful GCP deployment/ops bullet if it exists.
[SHOULD] Add one concrete scope metric to the TikTok first bullet so the newest experience reads as infrastructure at scale, not just percentage improvement.
[NICE] Keep the rest of the structure, summary framing, and immutable fields unchanged.
